ops.title: cookiesync captures localStorage/sessionStorage (not just cookies) for localStorage-auth sites like Faye; Browserbase needs -p on EVERY call

ops.body:
cookiesync now captures **web storage** (localStorage + sessionStorage) alongside cookies so localStorage-auth sites (e.g. Faye / app.findfaye.com — token lives in localStorage key `auth`, NOT a cookie, NOT IndexedDB) drive logged-in in `agent-browser-with-cookies`. Shipped 2026-07-07.

- **cookiesync (Go, committed a2f949bd, CI green):** new `internal/cookie/webstorage.go` goleveldb reader (copy-first, LOCK-safe, Chrome String16 marker-byte decode 0x00=UTF-16LE/0x01=Latin-1). `--format playwright` now emits real `origins[].localStorage`; new `--format webstorage` sidecar also carries sessionStorage (Playwright storageState has no sessionStorage slot). New consent-gated LOCAL-only `get_web_storage` RPC reuses the get_cookies grant (no extra tap; key discarded — web storage is unencrypted). **IndexedDB deferred**: goleveldb can't open it (custom `idb_cmp1` comparator + V8 structured-clone values).
- **skill agent-browser-with-cookies 0.8.0 (cc-skills, committed 1bc63e1e):** local `state load` restores localStorage automatically (no command change). Browserbase seeds per-origin via `storage local/session set` + reload.
- **Browserbase reuse FIXED via `ab` (skill 0.9.0, cc-skills, SHIPPED).** Root cause: agent-browser's built-in `-p browserbase` creates a NEW cloud session per CLI call, no reconnect → follow-ups land on `about:blank`. `ab` (`bin/ab`, replaces `agent-browser-bb`) keeps ONE keepAlive Browserbase session per caller: tag = `cookiesync requestor` slug (userMetadata.ab), found via `GET /sessions?status=RUNNING&q=user_metadata['ab']:'<slug>'`, reconnected with **`agent-browser connect "<connectUrl>"`** (NOT `--cdp` — that's port-only; re-connect resets the page so connect only when `agent-browser session list` lacks the slug), released on `ab close` via `POST /sessions/{id} {status:REQUEST_RELEASE}`. Key facts: create needs NO projectId (default project); keepAlive REQUIRED (paid plan) or session dies on disconnect; userMetadata values must be alnum/dash (spaces/`·` → 400, so use the SLUG not the display tag); connectUrl comes from the API (list omits it, GET /sessions/{id} has it), never construct `wss://connect.<region>`; Browserbase DOES have per-plan concurrent caps (Free 3/Dev 25). ab feeds the key to curl via stdin `--config -` (not argv), and a release-trap frees a just-created session if connect fails. Live-verified: reuse, single session, close-releases, orphan-release, Faye→/home on cloud IP. Chose cdp-wrapper over an agent-browser browser.provider PLUGIN (which IS a real capability, but wrapper is simpler). Also NEW: **`cookiesync requestor`** CLI (in v0.10.0) prints the stable requestor token (pid fallback) — the single identity source ab reuses.
- **cookiesync v0.10.0 RELEASED** (2026-07-08). Carries `get_web_storage` + `cookiesync requestor`.
- **Stale-daemon incident RESOLVED (2026-07-10, v0.10.1):** the real gap was never a missing upgrade — brew cask upgrades swap the binary but never touch launchd, so yasyf-home's `helper-serve` kept running pre-0.10.0 code for two days after the 0.10.0 upgrade and rejected `get_web_storage` (`cookiesync: unknown method`). Diagnosis key: that error means the RPC round-tripped to a live daemon whose dispatcher lacks the method — check the daemon process's `lstart` against the Caskroom install time; `cookiesync doctor` shows all-green for a stale daemon (no version/capability check, declined as out of scope). **v0.10.1 SHIPPED + live-verified on both Macs:** the cask now has a goreleaser-generated `postflight` (`hooks.post.install` in .goreleaser.yaml — never hand-edit the tap file) running `launchctl kickstart -k gui/#{Process.uid}/com.github.yasyf.synckit.helper.cookiesync`, `must_succeed: false` so first-install/headless are quiet no-ops; upgrades now self-deploy (helper lstart moved to the upgrade moment on both Macs with zero manual launchctl). Every helper restart drops the warm key cache → one fresh routed tap on next use. abwc recipe E2E re-verified on yasyf-home (Apple developer.apple.com/account signed in).

- **v0.10.2 RELEASED (2026-07-10):** cask postflight now also strips `com.apple.quarantine` (`xattr -dr` before the kickstart) — cookiesync was the one bare-binary cask in the fleet missing the scaffold-default strip. Upgraded + live-verified on this Mac; yasyf-home picks it up on its next brew upgrade. Doctor note: for ~2-3 min after the postflight kickstart restarts the daemon, the key-cache `auth_status` probe can FAIL with a 2s i/o timeout (startup converge + first SE unwrap under screen sharing) — transient, re-run doctor; steady state is the OK-with-note line.

Related: [[cookiesync-architecture]], [[agent-browser-state-handoff]], [[cookiesync-consent-routing-unified]].
